diagnosis: The search runs against index=*, which forces Splunk to scan every index on the instance instead of only the relevant data.
patch: Replaced index=* with index=main to bound the search to one index.
rationale: Pin the search to a specific index (index=main) so only relevant buckets are read.

diagnosis: The search declares no earliest/latest time bound, so it can scan the full retention window and do far more work than the question requires.
patch: Added an explicit earliest=-24h window to bound the time range.
rationale: Add an explicit earliest=-24h window so the search reads a bounded time range.

text: The | sort command has no result limit, so Splunk must buffer and order the entire result set, which is memory- and time-intensive on large searches.

message: | sort has no result limit, so the entire result set is buffered and ordered.
summary: Capped | sort with an explicit 1000-row limit.

message: | sort has no result limit, so the entire result set is buffered and ordered.

diagnosis: The | sort command has no result limit, so Splunk must buffer and order the entire result set, which is memory- and time-intensive on large searches.
patch: Capped | sort with an explicit 1000-row limit.
rationale: Cap the sort with an explicit limit (| sort 1000 ...) to bound memory and time.
message: | sort has no result limit, so the entire result set is buffered and ordered.
